title: Signal Dry Bulk Weekly Report
summary:
Main
We are experiencing an ongoing upturn of dry bulk freight rates, while Russia has moved forward with the ban on exports for specific commodities and countries. The economy ministry has also warned of further measures that could restrict foreign ships from Russian ports. The Russia ban will be imposed on countries that have committed unfriendly actions to Russia.

children.title: Main
children.summary: We are experiencing an ongoing upturn of dry bulk freight rates, while Russia has moved forward with the ban on exports for specific commodities and countries. The economy ministry has also warned of further measures that could restrict foreign ships from Russian ports.

children.title: SECTION 1 - FREIGHT - Market Rates ($/t) - Firmer
children.summary:
Image reference: ch1.png | assets/2022-03-16_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_ch1_6c06669250fd.png
Capesize Brazil to NChina rates increased to levels above $30/t this week, almost 5 points up from previous week, while there is a constant accelerated trend since Week 7. The turnaround is remarkable compared to the low of Week 3 $18/t.

children.title: Number of Vessels - Decreasing
children.summary:
Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas
Image reference: ch2.png | assets/2022-03-16_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_ch2_874dabf8ff0e.png
The ballasters' view has recorded a firm decrease in the Panamax segment, while the number of ballasters is aggressively increasing in the handysize and supramax segment. In the Capesize segment, the number of vessels sailing in ballast status is now near to the one-year average of 80 vessels for almost three consecutive weeks, while in the Panamax, the number dropped to 86 vessels compared to the peak of 156 vessels at Week 5.

children.title: Decreasing
children.summary:
Image reference: ch3.png | assets/2022-03-16_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_ch3_ea7e64567df8.png
The dry bulk demand evolution keeps growing in the Panamax and supramax vessel segments. It is interesting to see that demand growth in the handysize segment is evolving significantly at lower volumes than in the supramax segment.

children.title: Dry bulk ships congested around Chinese ports
section_path: Dry bulk ships congested around Chinese ports
children.section_path_text: Dry bulk ships congested around Chinese ports
children.summary:
Image reference: ch4.png | assets/2022-03-16_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_ch4_c7509126e1ad.png
The number of ship congestion is on an increasing trend for three successive weeks. The levels have surpassed the minimum of 860 vessels since Week 8, while the overall trend is still below the record highs witnessed last quarter of the previous year.
